name: Eli Lilly and Co
responsibilities:
• Lead frontend architecture and technical direction for enterprise web platforms
  • Own UI initiatives end-to-end, aligning with product roadmap and business goals
  • Define performance-focused architecture to improve responsiveness and long-term scalability
  • Develop internal tooling and AI-enabled solutions to standardize and accelerate UI development
  • Partner with product, design, and backend teams to deliver scalable frontend systems

projects.title: Marketplace
projects.softwareTech: React,Typescript, CSS, HTML5, Jest, React Testing library, MFE, Node.js, Python, Docker
projects.description: Marketplace is the front end for Enterprise Data, where users can take advantage of services Enterprise Data provides. This includes ingesting data into the Enterprise Data Backbone, initiating Data Projects, Connecting those Projects to the Analytics Work Bench (AWB), and searching for Marketplace Entries representing data that is available for users, enabling users to ask for access to that data.

projects.title: One Lilly Data
projects.softwareTech: Python, FastMCP, FastAPI, Neo4j, PostgreSQL, Redis, Docker, Kubernetes, OpenShift (Kubed), Cloudflare, Azure AD, OPA, OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, spaCy
projects.description:
One Lilly Data is an AI-native data platform that lets Claude.ai users discover, access, and query enterprise datasets through natural language — with full compliance enforcement at every step. The platform is built as a distributed MCP (Model Context Protocol) architecture with four core services:

CoC MCP Server — A thin MCP routing layer that bridges Claude.ai (Web + Desktop) to Lilly's internal data services. Authenticates users via Azure AD with OBO token exchange, then dispatches tool calls across three toolkits: Discover (graph search), Ask (SQL/NL queries), and Access (compliance checks). Includes circuit breakers, retry logic, rate limiting, Redis session persistence, and full audit logging.

Graph MCP Server — An MCP server backed by Neo4j that exposes the Lilly Enterprise Knowledge Graph. Provides 6 tools: entity search, lookup, listing, relationship traversal, N-hop neighborhood exploration, and shortest-path finding across 9 node types and 27 edge types.

Gateway Proxy — A FastAPI gateway that validates Access Broker compliance tokens, auto-classifies queries as SQL or natural language using spaCy heuristics, enforces read-only SQL guards (blocks DML/DDL/DCL), and routes NLP questions to Cortex Text2SQL or Cortex V2 inference for execution against enterprise PostgreSQL databases.

Access Broker — A compliance backend implementing a deterministic 12-stage access control pipeline: user profile validation, instant access scoring, device fingerprinting, origin validation, MFA re-confirmation, OPA authorization, LDS training verification, new-user detection, scope enforcement, and rate limiting. Every stage is fail-fast with a full audit trail. REST + gRPC interfaces.
